Hello, IέΑβm Azmat

IέΑβm a software engineer writing in public about what I build, break, measure, and learn.

This site is my working notebook for the technical side of life: performance investigations, architecture tradeoffs, deployment lessons, and the odd side project that teaches me something useful.

IέΑβm less interested in polished thought leadership than in honest field notes. If a post lives here, it usually came from a real incident, a stubborn bottleneck, or a build that finally started making sense after enough iteration.

Read more about me
azmat.devx

Personal writing about engineering work, systems thinking, and the technical details behind projects that survive contact with reality.
